title: DDE Syntax Rules
text: DDE Syntax Rules When you access Uniface as a DDE server, you must observe the following syntax rules for request transactions: The service name must be the name of the application as returned by $applname . If no service is specified, the default is "uniface" . The topic name must match the current component. The first part of the item string is a field name or comma-separated list of field names. This is optionally followed by an entity specification. After the entity name, you can specify the occurrence or occurrences that should be accessed. Item name syntax is shown below: Syntax Meaning field Single field in current occurrence. If no entity is specified, the normal Uniface binding rules apply. field1,field2,field3.entity field1, field2, and field3 of current occurrence of entity. *.entry All fields of current occurrence of entity. The entity specification is not optional when you use the "*" syntax for fields. field1,field2.entity[n] field1 and field2 of occurrence n of entity. field1,field2.entity[n: m ] field1 and field2 of occurrence n through m of entity. *.entity[*] Every database field of all occurrences of entity. If links are pasted from the clipboard (for example, with the Paste Link command), the "field.entity" syntax is used automatically. Related Links Uniface as a DDE Server When Uniface acts as a DDE server, the DDE link must be defined in the client application. $applname Return the name of the Application Shell.
